signatures.name: SQL Injection — UNION SELECT
signatures.description: Detects UNION-based injection used to dump rows from other tables. The attacker appends UNION SELECT to piggyback their own query onto the app's query.

signatures.name: SQL Injection — Boolean Blind
signatures.description: Detects boolean-based blind injection (OR 1=1, AND 1=1). Attackers inject always-true conditions to bypass login checks or force extra rows to be returned.

signatures.name: SQL Injection — Dangerous Stored Procedures
signatures.description: Detects calls to SQL Server built-in procedures that can execute OS commands (xp_cmdshell) or run arbitrary SQL (sp_executesql / EXEC). These lead to full server compromise.

signatures.name: XSS — Script Tag Injection
signatures.description: Detects raw <script> tags in the URL. If the server reflects the URL back into a page without escaping, the browser will run the attacker's JavaScript.

signatures.name: XSS — Inline Event Handler Injection
signatures.description: Detects HTML event attributes (onerror=, onload=, onclick=...) and protocol handlers (javascript:, vbscript:) injected into URLs to execute JavaScript without a <script> tag.

signatures.name: XSS — Dangerous JavaScript Calls
signatures.description: Detects JavaScript function calls frequently used in XSS payloads to pop alerts, steal cookies, or redirect the browser.

signatures.name: Directory Traversal — Dot-Dot-Slash
signatures.description: Detects ../ sequences in the URL path. Each ../ moves one directory up. Attackers chain them to climb out of the web root and read arbitrary files.

signatures.name: Directory Traversal — URL-Encoded Variants
signatures.description: Detects URL-encoded (%2e%2e%2f) and double-encoded (%252e%252e) forms of ../ that are sometimes used to bypass server-side path filters.

signatures.name: Directory Traversal — Sensitive System Files
signatures.description: Detects direct access to well-known sensitive system files. Even without a traversal prefix, requests for /etc/passwd or boot.ini are always suspicious.

signatures.name: Directory Traversal — Hidden Config Files
signatures.description: Detects probes for hidden configuration files (.htaccess, .env, .git/config) that often contain credentials, API keys, or server rules.
